heading: Reports and memoranda of understanding
label: 11152 Reports and memoranda of understanding
notes: Change of NameReference to Administrator of Veterans’ Affairs deemed to refer to Secretary of Veterans Affairs pursuant to , set out as a Department of Veterans Affairs Act note under , Veterans’ Benefits.section 10 of Pub. L. 100–527section 301 of Title 38

children.heading: Annual reports to Congress
children.label: a Annual reports to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall report to Congress, annually during the three years after , on the implementation of this chapter.November 14, 1986

children.heading: Memoranda of understanding
children.label: b Memoranda of understanding
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall seek to enter into memoranda of understanding with the Secretary of Defense and the Administrator of Veterans’ Affairs to apply the provisions of subchapter II of this chapter to hospitals and other facilities and health care providers under the jurisdiction of the Secretary or Administrator, respectively. The Secretary shall report to Congress, not later than two years after , on any such memoranda and on the cooperation among such officials in establishing such memoranda.November 14, 1986

children.heading: Memorandum of understanding with Drug Enforcement Administration
children.label: c Memorandum of understanding with Drug Enforcement Administration
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services shall seek to enter into a memorandum of understanding with the Administrator of Drug Enforcement relating to providing for the reporting by the Administrator to the Secretary of information respecting physicians and other practitioners whose registration to dispense controlled substances has been suspended or revoked under . The Secretary shall report to Congress, not later than two years after , on any such memorandum and on the cooperation between the Secretary and the Administrator in establishing such a memorandum.section 824 of title 21November 14, 1986
